# Copilot Instructions — Food Delivery Platform

## Project Summary
A polyglot microservices food delivery platform. An API Gateway (Kotlin/Spring Cloud Gateway) authenticates JWTs and routes to: Order Service (Java/Spring Boot + PostgreSQL + Stripe), Inventory Service (Python/FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Redis), Driver Service (Go + MongoDB), and Notification Service (TypeScript/Node.js + RabbitMQ + Twilio/SendGrid). A React/TypeScript/Vite/Tailwind frontend serves customer, restaurant, driver, and admin views.

## Commands
- **Build:** `docker-compose build`
- **Run:** `docker-compose up`
- **Install:** Not determinable from source
- **Test:** `cd order-service && mvn -B verify && cd ../inventory-service && pytest --tb=short -q && cd ../driver-service && go test ./... && cd ../notification-service && npx jest`
  - Inventory test command confirmed high-confidence from `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: `pytest --tb=short -q`

## Module Layout
| Directory | Language | Responsibility |
|---|---|---|
| `api-gateway/src/main/kotlin` | Kotlin | JWT auth filter, request routing |
| `order-service/src/main/java` | Java | Order lifecycle, Stripe payments, RabbitMQ publish |
| `inventory-service` | Python | Restaurant/menu CRUD, Redis stock reservation |
| `driver-service/cmd/driver` | Go | Driver CRUD, GPS location, availability |
| `notification-service/src` | TypeScript | RabbitMQ consumer, SMS/email dispatch |
| `frontend/src` | TypeScript | React SPA, Axios API client, role-based views |

## Restricted Paths — Never Modify
- `docker-compose.yml`
- `.env.example`
- `api-gateway/src/main/kotlin/com/fooddelivery/gateway/JwtAuthFilter.kt` — auth bypass risk
- `order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/service/StripePaymentGateway.java` — payment security
- `order-service/src/main/java/com/fooddelivery/order/model/OrderStatus.java` — transition validation; changes cascade to all services

## Key Domain Concepts
- **OrderStatus** — Enum with states `PENDING → ACCEPTED → PREPARING → READY_FOR_PICKUP → IN_TRANSIT → DELIVERED` (also `CANCELLED`, `REFUNDED`). Transitions are strictly enforced via `isValidTransition`; never skip states.
- **Stock Reservation** — Redis distributed lock in the inventory service preventing concurrent overselling. All stock-modifying code must acquire and release this lock.
- **Order Event** — RabbitMQ message on the `order.events` exchange emitted on every status change; consumed by the Notification Service. Exchange/routing key names are a shared contract.
- **Idempotency Key** — Composite `orderId + status` key in the Notification Service preventing duplicate SMS/email delivery. Currently in-memory; resets on restart.
- **X-Customer-Id** — HTTP header injected by the gateway from the JWT `sub` claim; downstream services trust it implicitly without re-validating.

## Top 3 Pitfalls
1. **Wrong language idioms per service.** Each service uses a different stack. Do not apply Spring patterns in Go, async SQLAlchemy patterns synchronously in Python, or Node patterns in Java.
2. **Skipping the Redis lock for stock changes.** Any code path that modifies `MenuItem.stock_quantity` without acquiring the distributed lock risks deadlocks or overselling.
3. **Breaking the X-Customer-Id contract.** The gateway sets this header; all downstream services read it. Renaming it in one place silently breaks authentication across every service.
